verses.text: A lamentation for Tyre, who boasted, 'I am of perfect beauty,' enthroned amid the seas. Pride in splendour. She gloried in a beauty that would not last.
A lamentation for Tyre, who boasted, 'I am of perfect beauty,' enthroned amid the seas. Pride in splendour. She gloried in a beauty that would not last.
A lamentation for Tyre, who boasted, 'I am of perfect beauty,' enthroned amid the seas. Pride in splendour. She gloried in a beauty that would not last.
A lamentation for Tyre, who boasted, 'I am of perfect beauty,' enthroned amid the seas. Pride in splendour. She gloried in a beauty that would not last.
Her timbers of fir, masts of cedar, oars of oak, sails of fine linen — a ship of magnificence. Crafted glory. Every part of her was choice, yet all was perishable.
Her timbers of fir, masts of cedar, oars of oak, sails of fine linen — a ship of magnificence. Crafted glory. Every part of her was choice, yet all was perishable.
Her timbers of fir, masts of cedar, oars of oak, sails of fine linen — a ship of magnificence. Crafted glory. Every part of her was choice, yet all was perishable.
Skilled mariners of Zidon and calkers of Gebal served her; all ships gathered to trade with her. A hub of commerce. The world's traffic flowed through her harbours.
Skilled mariners of Zidon and calkers of Gebal served her; all ships gathered to trade with her. A hub of commerce. The world's traffic flowed through her harbours.
Soldiers of Persia, Lud, and Phut manned her walls, hanging shield and helmet to 'set forth thy comeliness.' Borrowed strength. Her defences too were drawn from many lands.
Soldiers of Persia, Lud, and Phut manned her walls, hanging shield and helmet to 'set forth thy comeliness.' Borrowed strength. Her defences too were drawn from many lands.
Tarshish, Javan, and Tubal traded silver, iron, slaves, brass, and ivory with her. Vast merchandise. The riches of nations passed through Tyre's markets.
Tarshish, Javan, and Tubal traded silver, iron, slaves, brass, and ivory with her. Vast merchandise. The riches of nations passed through Tyre's markets.
Tarshish, Javan, and Tubal traded silver, iron, slaves, brass, and ivory with her. Vast merchandise. The riches of nations passed through Tyre's markets.
Tarshish, Javan, and Tubal traded silver, iron, slaves, brass, and ivory with her. Vast merchandise. The riches of nations passed through Tyre's markets.
Syria, Judah, and Damascus brought emeralds, wheat, honey, oil, and wine. Plenty from every side. Her wealth was gathered from all the surrounding peoples.
Syria, Judah, and Damascus brought emeralds, wheat, honey, oil, and wine. Plenty from every side. Her wealth was gathered from all the surrounding peoples.
Syria, Judah, and Damascus brought emeralds, wheat, honey, oil, and wine. Plenty from every side. Her wealth was gathered from all the surrounding peoples.
Dan, Javan, Dedan, Arabia, and Kedar traded cassia, cloth, lambs, and goats. Even the desert tribes. None were too distant to enrich her.
Dan, Javan, Dedan, Arabia, and Kedar traded cassia, cloth, lambs, and goats. Even the desert tribes. None were too distant to enrich her.
Dan, Javan, Dedan, Arabia, and Kedar traded cassia, cloth, lambs, and goats. Even the desert tribes. None were too distant to enrich her.
Merchants of Sheba and Asshur brought chief spices, precious stones, gold, and rich apparel. The finest of all lands. Tyre's glory was the gathered treasure of the world.
Merchants of Sheba and Asshur brought chief spices, precious stones, gold, and rich apparel. The finest of all lands. Tyre's glory was the gathered treasure of the world.
Merchants of Sheba and Asshur brought chief spices, precious stones, gold, and rich apparel. The finest of all lands. Tyre's glory was the gathered treasure of the world.
The ships of Tarshish sang of her, and she was glorious in the seas; but 'the east wind hath broken thee.' Sudden ruin. One storm of judgment sinks all her gathered wealth.
The ships of Tarshish sang of her, and she was glorious in the seas; but 'the east wind hath broken thee.' Sudden ruin. One storm of judgment sinks all her gathered wealth.
The ships of Tarshish sang of her, and she was glorious in the seas; but 'the east wind hath broken thee.' Sudden ruin. One storm of judgment sinks all her gathered wealth.
Her pilots cry, the mariners weep, casting dust on their heads and girding sackcloth for her. Universal mourning. The fall of the great draws bitter lament.
Her pilots cry, the mariners weep, casting dust on their heads and girding sackcloth for her. Universal mourning. The fall of the great draws bitter lament.
Her pilots cry, the mariners weep, casting dust on their heads and girding sackcloth for her. Universal mourning. The fall of the great draws bitter lament.
Her pilots cry, the mariners weep, casting dust on their heads and girding sackcloth for her. Universal mourning. The fall of the great draws bitter lament.
They lament, 'What city is like Tyrus, like the destroyed in the midst of the sea?' Her merchandise and people gone. Incomparable loss. The wonder of the seas becomes a byword of ruin.
They lament, 'What city is like Tyrus, like the destroyed in the midst of the sea?' Her merchandise and people gone. Incomparable loss. The wonder of the seas becomes a byword of ruin.
They lament, 'What city is like Tyrus, like the destroyed in the midst of the sea?' Her merchandise and people gone. Incomparable loss. The wonder of the seas becomes a byword of ruin.
The isles are astonished; the merchants hiss; she becomes 'a terror, and never shalt be any more.' Vanished glory. Earthly greatness ends in dread and nothingness.
